tools.name: enterprises.androidmanagementEnterprisesCreate
tools.description: Creates an enterprise. This is the last step in the enterprise signup flow. See also: SigninDetail

tools.name: enterprises.androidmanagementEnterprisesDevicesIssueCommand
tools.description: Issues a command to a device. The Operation resource returned contains a Command in its metadata field. Use the get operation method to get the status of the command.

tools.name: enterprises.androidmanagementEnterprisesDevicesList
tools.description: Lists devices for a given enterprise. Deleted devices are not returned in the response.

tools.name: enterprises.androidmanagementEnterprisesDevicesOperationsCancel
tools.description: Starts asynchronous cancellation on a long-running operation. The server makes a best effort to cancel the operation, but success is not guaranteed. If the server doesn't support this method, it returns google.rpc.Code.UNIMPLEMENTED. Clients can use Operations.GetOperation or other methods to check whether the cancellation succeeded or whether the operation completed despite cancellation. On successful cancellation, the operation is not deleted; instead, it becomes an operation with an Operation.error value with a google.rpc.Status.code of 1, corresponding to Code.CANCELLED.

tools.name: enterprises.androidmanagementEnterprisesEnrollmentTokensCreate
tools.description: Creates an enrollment token for a given enterprise. It's up to the caller's responsibility to manage the lifecycle of newly created tokens and deleting them when they're not intended to be used anymore. Once an enrollment token has been created, it's not possible to retrieve the token's content anymore using AM API. It is recommended for EMMs to securely store the token if it's intended to be reused.

tools.name: enterprises.androidmanagementEnterprisesEnrollmentTokensList
tools.description: Lists active, unexpired enrollment tokens for a given enterprise. The list items contain only a partial view of EnrollmentToken: all the fields but name and expiration_timestamp are empty. This method is meant to help manage active enrollment tokens lifecycle. For security reasons, it's recommended to delete active enrollment tokens as soon as they're not intended to be used anymore.

tools.name: enterprises.androidmanagementEnterprisesList
tools.description: Lists EMM-managed enterprises. Only BASIC fields are returned.

tools.name: enterprises.androidmanagementEnterprisesPoliciesList
tools.description: Lists policies for a given enterprise.

tools.name: enterprises.androidmanagementEnterprisesWebAppsList
tools.description: Lists web apps for a given enterprise.

tools.name: enterprises.androidmanagementEnterprisesWebTokensCreate
tools.description: Creates a web token to access an embeddable managed Google Play web UI for a given enterprise.

tools.name: openapi.previewSpec
tools.description: Preview an OpenAPI document before adding it as a source

tools.name: openapi.addSource
tools.description: Add an OpenAPI source and register its operations as tools
